sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Fund 12 months of part-time maintenance for Oura by TxPipe, including maintenance, community support, and AI-friendly documentation/integration resources.
Recipient: Intersect Treasury Reserve Smart Contract stake address stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v, for project-specific administration on behalf of TxPipe/Oura

Deliverables: Essential Oura codebase maintenance including dependency updates, Cardano protocol compatibility, performance improvements, bug fixing, and documentation., Community support including issue triage, review of external contributions, ecosystem-feedback-driven enhancements, and public communication., Upgrade Oura compatibility with AI-driven development workflows through AI-friendly documentation and integration resources., Use Intersect treasury management smart contracts with milestone-based disbursement controls and reporting obligations.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 540,750 ADA total, consisting of 525,000 ADA for Oura maintenance and enhancement and a 15,750 ADA Intersect Budget Administration fee.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The maintenance funding is described as 420,000 ADA plus a 105,000 ADA contingency reserve for a part-time maintainer over 12 months at an annual rate of $105,000 USD.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Oura is described as a Rust-native pipeline tool that connects to Cardano nodes, monitors blockchain events, and routes matching patterns to pluggable outputs.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, medium materiality): The document states Oura supports multiple data sources including N2C, N2N, S3, Mithril, and UTxO RPC, and many sinks including Kafka, Elasticsearch, AWS services, RabbitMQ, Redis, SQL databases, and webhooks.

Claim (adoption, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): The proposer states Oura has 654 commits, 288 stars, 79 forks, and 58 contributors, and is the most starred project in the TxPipe portfolio.
Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposer states Oura has been funded twice through Project Catalyst, in Fund 9 and Fund 13, for feature development.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposer states Oura has been funded twice through Project Catalyst, in Fund 9 and Fund 13, for feature development.

Independent evidence for: The proposer states TxPipe has received funding through 30 Project Catalyst proposals across Funds 9 through 14, with 26 successfully delivered and 4 currently under development and on schedule.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 540,750 ADA total, consisting of 525,000 ADA for Oura maintenance and enhancement and a 15,750 ADA Intersect Budget Administration fee." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposer states Oura has been funded twice through Project Catalyst, in Fund 9 and Fund 13, for feature development." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
